sound_type: near-inaudible low hoot -- soft, deep 'boop' at 300-450 Hz; barely audible at 30-50 meters; the quietest owl vocalization in North America; extreme ventriloquial effect; E=2 NEW SPS MINIMUM
ecosystem: Ponderosa pine forests, dry open montane forest. East side of Cascades. Summer breeder only (migratory, winters Mexico/Central America). Strictly insectivorous. The only small owl with dark eyes in North America.
season: Summer breeder only (May-September in PNW). Migratory -- winters in Mexico/Central America. Calling peaks May-July. Nocturnal.
family: Strigidae (10th consecutive: 135-144. BREAKS PICIDAE RECORD -- NEW ALL-TIME FAMILY STREAK)

coordinates.description: Flammulated Owls breed in ponderosa pine forests east of the Cascades in Washington -- Okanogan, Wenatchee, Kittitas areas.

taxonomy_note.description: First Flammulated Owl entry. TENTH consecutive Strigidae -- BREAKS Picidae nonet record. New all-time family streak. Seventh genus (Psiloscops, monotypic). Only small owl with dark eyes. Strictly insectivorous. Migratory. E=2 NEW SPS MINIMUM.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Strigidae: eleventh entry total. Ten consecutive at 135-144. ALL-TIME RECORD.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Psiloscops from Greek 'psilos' (bare, smooth) + 'scops' (owl) -- referring to the lack of ear tufts in some descriptions. flammeolus from Latin 'flammeus' (flame-colored), diminutive -- 'little flame-colored one.'
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Psiloscops -- first entry. Monotypic genus. Seventh genus in the Strigidae run.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Strigiformes. Eleventh entry. Ten consecutive at 135-144.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Strigidae BREAKS Picidae nonet record. Ten consecutive = new all-time family streak. Seven genera. Eight species. E=6 to E=2. 26:1 mass ratio.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Hoot: 300-450 Hz. Remarkably low for 54g body mass.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Primary hoot: 300-450 Hz. Lower than expected for body size. Similar frequency to Long-eared Owl but at a fraction of the amplitude.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual hoot: 0.2-0.3 seconds. Repeated at 2-3 second intervals. Calling bouts: 30+ minutes continuous.
song_metrics.note_structure: SINGLE HOOT: One soft, low-pitched 'boop' per interval. No phrase structure. No variation. Maximum simplicity.
song_metrics.note_types: Hoot (primary, near-inaudible), trill (rare, courtship), bark (alarm, rare).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: No phrase structure. Single note repeated at 2-3 second intervals. The absence of structure is extreme.
song_metrics.timbre: Soft, muffled, hollow. Like a distant heartbeat. The softness is the defining timbral quality.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=2. NEW SPS MINIMUM. Barely audible at 30-50 meters. Inaudible in any wind or ambient noise. The softest vocalization in the engine.
song_metrics.tempo: ~0.3-0.5 notes per second (1 note every 2-3 seconds). Slower than the Pygmy-Owl. The slowest calling rate in the run.
song_metrics.context: Territorial advertisement, courtship. Males call from ponderosa pine canopy after full darkness. May-July peak.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. The near-inaudible hoot is consistent across the species' range.
song_metrics.key_property: NEAR-INAUDIBLE HOOT -- E=2 NEW SPS MINIMUM. 300-450 Hz, barely audible at 30m. BREAKS PICIDAE RECORD (10th consecutive Strigidae). 7th genus (Psiloscops). Only small owl with dark eyes. Insect-only diet. Migratory. Ponderosa pine specialist. East of Cascades. The quietest voice in the engine.

plumage.overall: Gray-brown with rufous scapulars (the 'flame' markings that give the species its name). DARK EYES (brown-black iris). Short, barely visible ear tufts. Small size.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: DARK EYES. The only small North American owl with dark (not yellow) eyes. Immediately diagnostic. Rufous scapulars. Tiny size.
plumage.flight_pattern: Moth-like, buoyant. Short flights from perch to perch. Hawking for flying insects.

physical_description.overall: Length: 15-17 cm. Weight: 43-63 g (average ~54g). Wingspan: 35-40 cm. Among the smallest owls in North America. Similar size to Pygmy-Owl but with different proportions (shorter tail).
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Dark eyes, rufous scapulars, tiny size. In ponderosa pine habitat east of the Cascades, a small owl with dark eyes is unambiguously a Flammulated Owl.
physical_description.field_id: Listen for an extremely soft, low-pitched 'boop' in ponderosa pine forest at night. If you can hear it, conditions are very quiet. Look for dark eyes to confirm identity. Summer only in PNW.

nodes.title: BREAKS Picidae Record -- Ten Consecutive Strigidae (NEW ALL-TIME)
nodes.description: Ten consecutive Strigidae entries (135-144). Seven genera. Eight species. New all-time longest family streak. E=6 to E=2 range. 26:1 mass ratio. The most comprehensive single-family documentation in the engine.

nodes.title: E=2 -- New SPS Minimum
nodes.description: Near-inaudible at 30-50 meters. 300-450 Hz. Most extreme ventriloquial effect. At the threshold of human perception. No previous entry below E=3. The quietest voice in the engine.

nodes.title: Dark Eyes -- Unique Among Small Owls
nodes.description: Only small North American owl with dark (brown-black) eyes. All others have yellow eyes. Linked to insectivorous diet. Monotypic genus (Psiloscops). Immediately diagnostic field mark.

nodes.title: Ponderosa Pine Specialist -- East-Side Owl
nodes.description: Open ponderosa pine forests east of the Cascades. Summer breeder only. Migratory (winters Mexico/Central America). Strictly insectivorous. First east-side owl in the run.

nodes.title: Energy Descent Complete (E=6 to E=2)
nodes.description: Strigidae energy arc: E=4, 5, 4, 6, 4, 5, 3, 3, 3, 2. Peak at degree 138 (E=6 caterwauling). Minimum at degree 144 (E=2 near-inaudible). The journey from maximum volume to near-silence.

cross_references.relationship: ENERGY EXTREMES! E=6 (138) to E=2 (144). The loudest and quietest entries in the Strigidae run. Maximum to minimum in six degrees.
MASS EXTREMES! GHO (~1400g) to Flammulated (~54g) = 26:1 ratio. Largest to smallest across ten consecutive degrees.
PREVIOUS DEGREE! Pygmy-Owl (E=3, diurnal) to Flammulated (E=2, nocturnal). Energy descent continues. Both tiny but radically different strategies.
DECTET COMPLETE! Ten consecutive Strigidae. BREAKS Picidae nonet. Seven genera. Eight species. E=6 to E=2. 26:1 mass ratio. All-time record.
